answer:
\boxed{The Burning of the Library of Alexandria}

Brautigan’s poem *"The Burning of the Library of Alexandria"* (from *The Abortion: An Historical Romance*) reimagines the destruction of the library as a violent, sexualized act—symbolizing the erasure of knowledge and culture by a monstrous figure. The "evil sherman" (a pun on "Sherman," referencing General William Tecumseh Sherman’s destructive march) embodies historical violence and the loss of innocence ("clothes that had no childhood"). This interpretation critiques both literal destruction and the metaphorical silencing of ideas.
